creator: cy@FreeBSD.org
text:
I still need the id string from your makefile. I cannot reproduce your problem locally here unless I uninstall openssl, which I patched the port in r434564 to flag itself as borken and fail to build. It should not include /usr/include/openssl/evp.h. Instead it should include /usr/local/include/openssl/evp.h. It is using openssl from base, which does not work, instead of using openssl or libressl from ports in $LOCALBASE.

1. Please post the id string for your Makefile.
2. Please post the contents of /etc/make.conf.
3. Pleases post the output in config.log.

Regardless of your openssl/libressl options and ports installed, it is using openssl from base which results in this error. Especially important is the output in config.log. It may have a clue why at your site it is preferring to use openssl in base. Make.conf may give us a clue.

The id string from Makefile should be greater than or equal to r434564. It should contain the following (which is why I ask for that):

Index: Makefile
===================================================================
--- Makefile	(revision 434563)
+++ Makefile	(revision 434564)
@@ -56,6 +56,7 @@
 SSL_CONFIGURE_ON=	--with-openssl-incdir=${OPENSSLINC} \
 			--with-openssl-libdir=${OPENSSLLIB}
 SSL_CONFIGURE_OFF=	--without-crypto
+SSL_BROKEN_OFF=		Includes OpenSSL when deselected
 
 THREADS_CONFIGURE_ENABLE=	thread-support
 THREADS_CONFIGURE_WITH=		threads
